questionText: A curve is defined by $x = t^{2} - 4t + 3$ and $y = t + 2$. Find the coordinates of the points where the curve crosses the $y$-axis.

steps.description: State the condition for the y-axis
steps.explanation: The $y$-axis is the set of points with zero horizontal displacement, so the curve meets it where $x = 0$.

steps.description: Set the x equation to zero
steps.explanation: Only the $x$-coordinate must vanish, so we work with the $x$ equation, producing a quadratic in $t$.
Two numbers multiplying to $3$ and adding to $-4$ are $-1$ and $-3$.
These parameter values place the point exactly on the $y$-axis. Both are valid.
The $y$ equation gives the height of the curve at this parameter value.
Combining $x = 0$ with this height gives the first crossing.
The same substitution for the second value of $t$.
This is the other point where the curve meets the $y$-axis.
The curve meets the $y$-axis at these two points.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The curve meets the $y$-axis at $(0,\,3)$ (when $t = 1$) and $(0,\,5)$ (when $t = 3$).
